Package org.solenopsis.soap.tooling

Class FlowOrchestratedStage

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.tooling.FlowBaseElement
org.solenopsis.soap.tooling.FlowElement
org.solenopsis.soap.tooling.FlowNode
org.solenopsis.soap.tooling.FlowOrchestratedStage
public class FlowOrchestratedStage extends FlowNode

Java class for FlowOrchestratedStage complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="FlowOrchestratedStage">
   <complexContent>
     <extension base="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}FlowNode">
       <sequence>
         <element name="connector" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}FlowConnector" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="exitActionInputParameters" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}FlowStageStepExitActionInputParameter" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="exitActionName" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="exitActionOutputParameters" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}FlowStageStepExitActionOutputParameter" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="exitActionType" type="{urn:tooling.soap.sforce.com}InvocableActionType" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="exitConditionLogic" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="exitConditions" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}FlowCondition" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="faultConnector" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}FlowConnector" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="stageSteps" type="{urn:metadata.tooling.soap.sforce.com}FlowStageStep" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
       </sequence>
     </extension>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

  • Method Details

    • getConnector

      public FlowConnector getConnector()
      Gets the value of the connector property.
      Returns:
      possible object is FlowConnector

    • setConnector

      public void setConnector(FlowConnector value)
      Sets the value of the connector property.
      Parameters:
      value - allowed object is FlowConnector

    • getExitActionInputParameters

      public List<FlowStageStepExitActionInputParameter> getExitActionInputParameters()
      Gets the value of the exitActionInputParameters property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the exitActionInputParameters property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getExitActionInputParameters().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list FlowStageStepExitActionInputParameter

      Returns:
      The value of the exitActionInputParameters property.

    • getExitActionName

      public String getExitActionName()
      Gets the value of the exitActionName property.
      Returns:
      possible object is String

      Sets the value of the exitActionName property.
      Parameters:
      value - allowed object is String

    • getExitActionOutputParameters

      public List<FlowStageStepExitActionOutputParameter> getExitActionOutputParameters()
      Gets the value of the exitActionOutputParameters property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the exitActionOutputParameters property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getExitActionOutputParameters().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list FlowStageStepExitActionOutputParameter

      Returns:
      The value of the exitActionOutputParameters property.

    • getExitConditions

      public List<FlowCondition> getExitConditions()
      Gets the value of the exitConditions property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the exitConditions property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getExitConditions().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list FlowCondition

      Returns:
      The value of the exitConditions property.

    • getStageSteps

      public List<FlowStageStep> getStageSteps()
      Gets the value of the stageSteps property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the stageSteps property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getStageSteps().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list FlowStageStep

      Returns:
      The value of the stageSteps property.
